title: "won't recognise NTFS"
date: 2007-11-07
forum: General Help

### Post by haunticious on 2007-11-07
Hey, I've been using ubuntu for a few months now and i've recently reinstalled feisty. I had my other partition which is NTFS working before using ntfs-3g and it was fine, i had read and write but i've reinstalled feisty and now it doesn't work. I've installed ntfs-3g but it doesn't work now. Here is what my fstab says:

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda2 :
UUID=1a8e5fec-a640-4b2e-bb15-51972fa124c1 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda5 :
UUID=e0383632-f1da-47b6-b4d8-eaa75a927887 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/Windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0

any suggestions?:confused:

---

### Post by taurus on 2007-11-07
What is the error message when you try to mount it by hand from a terminal?

Applications -> Accessories -> Terminal
```
sudo mount -t ntfs /dev/hda1 /media/Windows
```

---

### Post by namanbagga on 2007-11-07
Try this
```
sudo apt-get install ntfs-config
```

---

### Post by haunticious on 2007-11-08
ahh don't worry everybody, i booted into windows and it did a chkdsk, after that it mounted perfectly..thanks for the help though
Daniel

---
